items.headline: भारत की मेघना सज्जनार ने एशियन शूटिंग चैंपियनशिप में जीता ब्रॉन्ज़ मेडल

summaryParagraphs: अब इस लाइव ब्लॉग को रोकने का वक़्त आ गया है. बीबीसी संवाददाता चंदन कुमार जजवाड़े को दीजिए इजाज़त. कल एक नए लाइव ब्लॉग के साथ फिर हाज़िर होंगे. बीबीसी हिन्दी पर मौजूद कुछ अहम ख़बरों को पढ़ने के लिए आप उनके साथ लगे लिंक पर क्लिक कर सकते हैं. - रूसी तेल पर पीयूष गोयल ने नहीं दिया कोई जवाब, जानें कौन से अमेरिकी उत्पादों पर टैरिफ़ हटा देगा भारत - पटना में गर्ल्स हॉस्टल के हालात : 'दिल्ली महंगा था, लेकिन यहां तो जान पर ही ख़तरा है' - मध्य प्रदेश: मधुमक्खियों से 25 बच्चों को बचाने के लिए अपनी जान गंवाने वाली कंचन बाई - 'हम सर कटाने को तैयार', राजस्थान में खेजड़ी के पेड़ काटे जाने के ख़िलाफ़ धरना For the full story and latest updates, read the complete article. items.headline: Japan votes in key test for PM Takaichi as heavy snow hits turnout
whyItMatters: Japanese voters were casting ballots on Sunday in an election expected to hand Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi a resounding win, although record snowfall in parts of the country could keep many voters a
summaryParagraphs: Japanese voters were casting ballots on Sunday in an election expected to hand Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi a resounding win, although record snowfall in parts of the country could keep many voters at home. The conservative coalition of Takaichi, the nation's first female leader, could win more than 300 of the 465 seats in the lower house of parliament, according to multiple opinion polls, a large gain from the 233 it is defending. If the coalition of Takaichi's Liberal Democratic Party with the Japan Innovation Party, known as Ishin, wins 310 seats, it would be able to override the opposition-controlled upper chamber. She has vowed to step down if the coalition loses its majority. TAKAICHI RIDES 'SANAKATSU' WAVE AMONG YOUNG VOTERS Takaichi, 64, who became prime minister in October after being selected LDP leader, sought a mandate from voters in a rare winter election as she rides a wave of popularity. With a straight-talking style and an image as a hard worker that have won her suppo For the full story and latest updates, read the complete article. Source: Web.

items.headline: Zelenskyy says US set June deadline for Ukraine-Russia war deal
whyItMatters: UPDATED: Feb 8, 2026 03:46 IST Posted By: Nitish K Singh The United States has proposed a June deadline for Ukraine and Russia to reach an agreement to end the nearly four-year-long war, Ukrainian Pre
summaryParagraphs: UPDATED: Feb 8, 2026 03:46 IST Posted By: Nitish K Singh The United States has proposed a June deadline for Ukraine and Russia to reach an agreement to end the nearly four-year-long war, Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said, as fresh Russian strikes on energy infrastructure forced Ukraine’s nuclear power plants to reduce output on Saturday. Speaking to reporters on Friday, Zelenskyy said Washington was pushing for a settlement “by the beginning of this summer” and may apply pressure on both sides to meet the timeline. “The Americans are proposing the parties end the war by the beginning of this summer and will probably put pressure on the parties precisely according to this schedule,” he said, adding that the Trump administration was likely to intensify efforts if the deadline is not met. Zelenskyy also said the US had suggested holding the next round of trilateral talks in the United States next week for the first time, possibly in Miami, and that For the full story and latest updates, read the complete article. Source: Web.

summaryParagraphs: Duty concessions on American whiskey and wine are unlikely to significantly impact Indian companies due to minimum import prices and low existing volumes. American whiskey holds less than 0.1% of the Indian market, with distinct flavors appealing to a niche segment. whyItMatters: India and the US have reached a breakthrough interim trade agreement, a significant shift from recent tensions. This deal, a precursor to a larger pact, offers advantages to India's MSME and labor-int
summaryParagraphs: India and the US have reached a breakthrough interim trade agreement, a significant shift from recent tensions. This deal, a precursor to a larger pact, offers advantages to India's MSME and labor-intensive sectors. Negotiators successfully resisted pressure on sensitive agricultural imports, a move likely to please the US president's base ahead of mid-term polls. For the full story and latest updates, read the complete article. Source: Times of India. For the full story and latest updates, read the complete article. Source: Web.

summaryParagraphs: India aims to import $500 billion in US goods over five years, but success hinges on American suppliers meeting demand. items.headline: 'Politically motivated': Sonia Gandhi slams plea over 1980 voter list inclusion
whyItMatters: Sonia Gandhi Saturday termed a criminal revision petition against a magisterial order as "politically motivated and frivolous." The petition alleges her name was included in electoral rolls in 1980 us
summaryParagraphs: Sonia Gandhi Saturday termed a criminal revision petition against a magisterial order as "politically motivated and frivolous." The petition alleges her name was included in electoral rolls in 1980 using forged documents, despite her acquiring Indian citizenship later. Gandhi argued citizenship and electoral roll issues fall outside criminal court jurisdiction. For the full story and latest updates, read the complete article. Source: Times of India. items.headline: American Air CEO Agrees to Meet Pilots Union After Criticism
whyItMatters: (Bloomberg) -- American Airlines Group Inc. Chief Executive Office Robert Isom on Saturday responded to a flood of concerns from pilots about the company’s underperformance, days after the union threa
summaryParagraphs: (Bloomberg) -- American Airlines Group Inc. Chief Executive Office Robert Isom on Saturday responded to a flood of concerns from pilots about the company’s underperformance, days after the union threatened to hold a vote of no confidence in management.  The board of the Allied Pilots Association said in a letter late Friday that it wants Nick Silva, the union’s president, to formally present its concerns to American’s directors, arguing that the airline lacks a clear identity or credible long-term strategy.  “Our airline is on an underperforming path and has failed to define an identity or a strategy to correct course,” the union wrote in the letter. Isom on Saturday responded in his own letter saying he’s “interested in addressing the issues that most concern our pilots,” including the handling of winter storms, attendance policies and the business plan to “return American to its rightful place atop the industry.”  Pilots have blamed leadership for what they described a For the full story and latest updates, read the complete article. Source: Web.
